ocr.text: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the expression levels of S100A2 in relation to various
ocular tissues, specifically the cornea and limbus.
Protein  Cornea  Limbus  SCC
S100A2  Negative  Plasma membrane of basal epithelial cells, decreasing progressively toward upper layers.  Overexpressed in all epithelial cells.
S100A6  Plasma membrane of all cells
S100A8/9  Negative  Located at the surface of the limbal epithelium.  Top layer cells of the epithelium, decreasing progressively toward the stroma and disappeared at the basal layer.
S100A10  Negative  Nucleus of limbal epithelial cells, decreasing progressively toward upper layers.  Nuclear staining of all epithelial cells.
S100A11  Negative  Plasma membrane staining of upper layer cells adjacent to the conjunctiva.  Uniform staining of all epithelial cells.
FUNDC1  P value
Low expression  High expression
Median OS (month)  40.9  36.7  0.045
Median DFS (month)  38.9  33.8  0.005
Antidiabetic medication  ORa(95 % CI)  P value  ORb(95 % CI)  P value  ORc(95 % CI)  P value
S100A2, a calcium-binding protein, is known to be overexpressed in all epithelial cells, suggesting its potential role in cellular
functions and responses. The analysis focuses on the correlation between S100A2 expression and the efficacy of antidiabetic
medications, as indicated by the various odds ratios (OR) and associated P values. Notably, the column labeled "Low
expression" highlights the prevalence of S100A2 in the plasma membrane of basal epithelial cells, which decreases
progressively toward the upper layers, potentially influencing the survival rates of patients with diabetes. Furthermore, the "5
years OS (%)" metric reveals significant survival outcomes, with an overall survival rate of 84% for patients exhibiting high
expression levels of S100A2, compared to 62.6% for those with low expression, underscoring the importance of this protein in
therapeutic contexts and patient prognoses.
